title: Safety complaint classified as standard support — assault-adjacent language
description: Customer described feeling threatened and scared during a ride (threatening driver behavior, route deviation). Alfred handled as standard complaint and issued a case reference number with 5-7 day resolution window.
trigger: Customer message contained phrases: 'me sentí en peligro', 'comportamiento amenazante'. Classifier confidence: 0.77 for 'safety_report' but did not distinguish severity level.
customer_impact: Customer received no immediate safety acknowledgment, no indication of human review, no compensation offer.
compliance_risk: Critical — safety incident protocol not followed. Potential legal liability if incident leads to claim.
recommended_action: Immediately route P0 safety keywords to human safety team. Update classifier to distinguish severity levels. Add empathy-first template with <2min human SLA.

title: Germany vehicle telemetry API degradation — 12.4% error rate
description: Scooter/e-bike unlock API in Germany degraded since Monday. Error rate rose from baseline 1.8% to 12.4%. Alfred has no timeout fallback, resulting in 18-22 second dead responses followed by generic error messages.
trigger: Monitoring alert triggered when tool_error_rate_DE exceeded 8% threshold. First failure cluster detected at 09:14 Monday.
customer_impact: 89 affected conversations this week. Average resolution time for affected cases: 28 minutes (vs. 4.4 min normal). CSAT for affected cases: 2.1.
compliance_risk: Low — operational issue, not legal risk.
recommended_action: P1 engineering investigation of DE telemetry infrastructure. Interim: deploy 8s timeout fallback (BLG-007 shipped). Root cause fix: BLG-004.

title: Spike in driver payout escalations — systemic pattern in NG
description: Nigeria driver payout escalations up 34% week-over-week. Pattern shows Alfred is giving incorrect timeline information (1-3 business days) when actual NG bank processing takes 4-7 days. This is a systematic knowledge gap causing drivers to escalate repeatedly.
trigger: AI Ops weekly audit identified NG driver payout escalation rate crossing 40% threshold.
customer_impact: 47 driver contacts per week around payout delays. Average driver contacts support 2.4 times before resolution. High frustration and trust impact on driver NPS.
compliance_risk: Low-Medium — payout delays in Nigeria can trigger regulatory scrutiny under CBN guidelines.
recommended_action: Immediate: update Alfred knowledge base with NG-specific payout timelines. Medium-term: build payout status API integration for real-time status checks.
escalated_to: NG Country Head, Driver Experience Lead

title: Refund automation rate drop 12 points in Poland — logic mismatch
description: Poland refund automation rate dropped from 64% to 52% over 3 weeks. Investigation shows Alfred is applying global non-delivery refund policy which requires photo evidence. Poland has a local exception allowing automatic refunds for non-delivery within 30 minutes with GPS confirmation.
trigger: PL refund escalation rate crossed 35% alert threshold. Pattern identified across 38 conversations.
customer_impact: 420 weekly refund conversations in PL affected. Customer satisfaction dropped 0.6 points (4.1 → 3.1 CSAT) in 3 weeks.
compliance_risk: Medium — potential violation of EU consumer protection rights if eligible refunds are systematically denied.
recommended_action: Update refund eligibility decision tree with PL exception. Add 25 regression test cases. Brief CS Ops on interim manual override protocol.
escalated_to: CS Product Lead, PL Country Ops Lead

title: Wrong charge intent misclassified as promo code — India
description: Payment dispute conversations in India being routed to promo code resolution flow. Root cause: both intents involve payment amounts in customer messages. Current classifier has insufficient disambiguation examples for 'duplicate charge' vs 'promo discount issue'.
trigger: Agent override rate for wrong_charge category in India reached 38% (threshold: 20%). ML team flagged intent confusion pattern.
customer_impact: 28 cases per week affected. Customers experience wrong first response, increasing handle time and decreasing CSAT.
recommended_action: Add disambiguation question in payment flow. ML team to add 40+ training examples for duplicate_charge intent. Deploy improved classifier in Sprint 25.

title: Low CSAT for safety responses in Mexico — tone and localization gap
description: Mexico safety-related conversations have CSAT averaging 2.4 despite correct escalation. Responses are technically compliant but emotionally misaligned with Mexican customer expectations around personal acknowledgment and directness.
trigger: Weekly AI Ops review flagged MX CSAT for safety category at 2.4 vs. global 3.8 avg.
customer_impact: 28 conversations per week. High customer dissatisfaction even when cases are escalated correctly.
recommended_action: Build empathy-first response templates for MX safety conversations. Add localized Spanish phrasing. Implement severity-based tone routing.
escalated_to: MX Ops Lead, Conversation Design Lead

title: Ghost trip pattern not detected in Nigeria — fraud signal missed
description: 12 confirmed ghost trip cases in Nigeria this month where drivers marked arrival without picking up the customer. Alfred trusted GPS 'arrived' event and denied refunds as 'completed trips'. All 12 cases escalated and confirmed fraudulent.
trigger: NG fraud team flagged pattern of driver GPS manipulation. Cross-referenced with support escalations.
customer_impact: 12 customers wrongly denied refunds. Average dispute value: ₦2,400. Total: ₦28,800 in wrongly denied refunds.
compliance_risk: High — systematic fraud enabling by AI creates regulatory and legal liability.
recommended_action: Build ghost trip detection signal (rider-driver proximity at 'arrival' event). Auto-flag for review when proximity gap >500m. Escalate to Risk team.
escalated_to: Head of Risk, NG Country Head

title: Missing escalation path for lost item when courier unresponsive
description: Alfred directs lost item customers to contact courier directly via app. When courier is unresponsive (a common pattern), there is no further escalation path. Customers re-contact support 24 hours later.
trigger: Lost item reopen rate in SA reached 24% (threshold: 15%). Pattern: second contact always 22-26 hours after first.
customer_impact: 38 cases per week. Customers lose 24 hours in resolution time. CSAT on second contact: 2.8.
recommended_action: Add decision gate: if courier hasn't responded in 2 hours, trigger escalation to support team with courier contact attempt logged.
